AR1.justification: Routed correctly with explicit rationale: today's daybook captures the cold-pickup and guardrail, followups.md gets the overdue update and cross-link, decisions.md is declined ('declining to send today is not a forward-binding commitment'), and observations.md is held back pending user confirmation — all consistent with the strict bars.

AR2.justification: Non-procedure scenario; AR2 is n/a per rubric.

AR3.justification: All references are relative with ISO-date anchors (AGENTS.md, followups.md, decisions.md, daybook/2026-04-21.md, daybook/2026-05-17.md, tmp/vendor-renegotiation/, observations.md) and resolve.

AR4.justification: Daybook and followups updates are coherent on re-run, but the Handoff section is explicitly left pending for later in the session — if the session ends without it being written, tomorrow's cold-pickup sees an incomplete daybook; a small forward-compatibility risk rather than a corruption risk.
